fullName: Engineering Recommendation G99: Requirements for the connection of generation equipment in parallel with public distribution networks
version: Guidance Document (Issue 1 Amdt 9 lineage; check ENA for current issue)
statusDate: Guidance published January 2023; G99 itself amended subsequently — always refer to latest ENA published issue
authority: Engineering Recommendation; referenced by the Distribution Code and gives effect to ENTSO-E Network Code RfG in GB
summary: The technical rulebook for connecting generation greater than 3.68 kW per phase to the GB distribution network. Covers the full range from rooftop solar through to utility-scale wind and battery storage connected at HV or EHV. Defines protection settings, fault ride-through, witness testing, and DNO application procedures. G98 is the sister document covering installations ≤3.68 kW per phase (Type A 'Fit and Inform').
scope: Type A (800 W – 1 MW): simple generators
Type B (1 MW – 10 MW): medium generators
Type C (10 MW – 50 MW): large generators
Type D (≥50 MW or connected at ≥110 kV): transmission-like requirements
LoM (Loss of Mains) protection — RoCoF and Vector Shift settings

keyParts.name: Form A1 — Application (Pre-connection)
keyParts.detail: DNO pre-approval application; submitted before connection.

keyParts.name: Form A2 — Commissioning Confirmation
keyParts.detail: Confirmation that protection settings meet requirements.

keyParts.name: Form A3 — Witness Test
keyParts.detail: DNO-witnessed commissioning of the protection system.

keyParts.name: Type A settings
keyParts.detail: Over/under voltage, over/under frequency, RoCoF, vector shift.

keyParts.name: Fault ride-through (Type B+)
keyParts.detail: Voltage profile curve during and after grid faults.
